entities.name: Floor
entities.notes: Static box, top face at y == 0. Collider half-extents (20, 1, 20), friction and restitution live in the runtime scenario mirror (positions here are the authored source of truth the mirror parses).

entities.name: Ramp
entities.notes: Static box ramp, rotated about z by -0.35 rad in the runtime mirror (entity transforms carry position only in schema v1; orientation, half-extents (6, 0.5, 4) and materials live in the mirror).

entities.name: Ball
entities.notes: THE PLAYER: a dynamic unit sphere. The input-bindings map WASD to move_x/move_y actions; the control system converts held move state into rolling impulses. Its floor impacts trigger the particle burst and the spatialized impact sound.

entities.name: Boulder-A
entities.notes: Dynamic sphere with an initial drift velocity (set in the runtime mirror) -- free physics activity alongside the player.

entities.name: Boulder-B
entities.notes: Dynamic sphere dropped over the ramp side so it lands on the incline and rolls off.

entities.name: FlagPole
entities.notes: The skeletal-animated prop: a 2-joint rig driven by anim/flag-wave.anim-graph.json. The gameplay couples its control parameter to the ball's accumulated impact count, so the flag transitions idle -> wave once the ball has bounced enough.

entities.name: ImpactBurst
entities.notes: The impact particle emitter anchor: rate 0 at rest; on a ball floor-impact the gameplay system repositions it to the ball and opens the rate for a short deterministic burst.

notes: The roll-3d arena. Authored positions are INTEGERS (exactly representable in Q16 fixed-point),
so the runtime scenario mirror converts them losslessly onto the deterministic sim path.
Collider shapes/sizes, masses, materials, initial velocities and the fixed per-tick system
order (input -> control -> physics3d -> impact -> particles -> animation) live in the runtime
mirror registered as session scenario 'roll-3d' (src/tests/integration/games/), which parses
THIS file for the entity layout -- renaming or moving an entity here fails the
game-smoke-roll-3d ctest, keeping the sample rot-proof beyond schema validity.
